content_markdown:
The graph shows the emission spectrum for a black body at absolute temperature $T_1$.

Which graph shows the emission spectrum for the same black body at an absolute temperature $T_2$ where $T_2 > T_1$? The original graph is shown as a dotted line.

diagram_metadata.description: A black body radiation curve showing relative intensity versus wavelength. The initial curve is labeled T1. Four options show a second curve labeled T2 relative to the dotted T1 curve.

vibe_explanation: According to Wien's displacement law, as temperature increases, the peak wavelength shifts to the left (shorter wavelengths). According to the Stefan-Boltzmann law, the total power radiated (area under the curve) increases significantly with temperature ($P \propto T^4$), so the peak intensity must be higher. Only graph A correctly shows both a higher peak and a shift toward shorter wavelengths.
